bullet_points: Pre-load all large numpy arrays (stacked signal maps, ground truth tSZ, kSZ, CMB, and CIB maps) into RAM at initialization as memory-mapped or fully-loaded arrays.
Implement a custom torch.utils.data.Dataset that indexes into these arrays per patch during __getitem__.
Compute and store per-patch local noise variance maps as auxiliary channels for the SO-band inputs.
Apply arcsinh transformation to tSZ and CIB maps for dynamic range compression.
Set DataLoader num_workers=8 to avoid thread oversubscription.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Data Preprocessing and Dataset Class Implementation

bullet_points: Construct a dual-branch U-Net architecture with separate encoders for SO-band and CIB-band inputs.
Ensure the encoders produce feature maps at multiple spatial resolutions (256, 128, 64, 32).
Implement gated cross-attention modules operating independently at each resolution level.
Use sigmoid-gated additive fusion for CIB branch feature injection into the SO branch decoder.
Concatenate noise variance maps as auxiliary input channels to the SO branch input only.
Implement the training loop using AdamW and a one-cycle learning rate scheduler.
Integrate the composite loss function: L1 reconstruction loss, spectral consistency loss (using utils.powers), and Sobel-filter edge loss.
Compute spectral consistency loss on CPU and transfer back to GPU, ensuring the computation graph is maintained.
Print loss weights (lambda_1, lambda_2, lambda_3) at start-up and per-epoch validation loss breakdowns.
Cap training at 30 epochs or use early stopping with patience=5; print final component-wise losses.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Model Training with Composite Loss Function

bullet_points: Compute RMSE, SSIM, and cross-correlation coefficients against ground truth tSZ maps.
Perform a null test by running the model on pure noise realizations to quantify hallucination.
Report the null test hallucination fraction as the RMS of model output on noise normalized by ground truth tSZ RMS.
Generate a single multi-panel figure containing: (a) ground truth tSZ patch, (b) model reconstruction, (c) residual map, and (d) angular power spectra of ground truth, reconstruction, and residual.
Train ablation models (no-CIB and no-spectral-loss) using identical hyperparameters and random seeds.
Implement Monte Carlo dropout at inference time (at least 20 passes) to generate pixel-wise uncertainty maps.
Visualize uncertainty maps alongside mean predictions and ground truth in a single composite figure.
Print RMSE, SSIM, and cross-correlation metrics for all models in a consolidated table to stdout.
Synthesize the quantitative results (RMSE, SSIM, spectral consistency) from the previous steps.
Interpret the impact of CIB auxiliary maps and the spectral loss on the reconstruction of baryonic pressure.
Discuss the angular scale dependence of reconstruction quality based on ell-dependent cross-correlation results.
Comment on the physical significance of the null test hallucination fraction relative to signal amplitude.
Provide a comprehensive summary of the research findings for the final report.
